sources_exist.note: Identity and all deterministic dataset inputs match exactly, and all seven URL occurrences resolved to accessible source content.

figures_match_sources.note: The accessible sources support general lab AI activity, selected TICC platform multiples, general owner demographics, and one geotechnical lab's unit fee schedule, but not the record's 12% adoption, 10-12-year analog, 35% lab-owner age, six consolidators, 7.5x buy multiple, 11.5x exit midpoint, or 70/20/10 pricing mix.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: All within-role fractions and capture/moat values are bounded and qualitatively respect the physical-testing constraint, but t50_years=10 lacks a supported adoption analog and begins from an unsupported 12% firm-adoption estimate, making the score-bearing A judgment unverifiable.

rubric_consistent.note: The record correctly bounds AI exposure to reporting, administration, and data reduction and marks the heterogeneous subsegments, but A, B, C, and M use unsupported quantified research. BORDERLINE: |S - pass cut 3.00| = 0.1457 makes these defects decision-critical. ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 3 inputs with quality ESTIMATE (current_adoption_pct, owners_60plus_pct, t50_years) is unresolved. ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, M, S, V is within tolerance and not independently disqualifying.

cross_checks_honest.note: The uplift, no-proxy, and durable-demand distinctions are candid, but pricing_model_deflation presents a 70/20/10 industry revenue mix as fact when the sole pricing URL is only one construction-materials laboratory's fee schedule. The terminal statement also asserts ISO 17025 requires a qualified human technical reviewer without a URL-bearing accreditation source in the record.

source_audits.note: This release concerns New Mountain's investment in SAM, a geospatial and inspection platform. It does not mention UES, Alliance, Pace, Element, Acuren, or Montrose, document their testing-lab add-ons, or support a count of six active 541380 consolidators.

Mainsights reports selected scaled TICC platform transactions, including ATS at 11.2x and GBA at 10.7x, and emphasizes material subsegment differences. It does not report an 8x small-cap median, US independent-lab add-on observations, or support a 7.5x buy multiple for $1M-$5M EBITDA labs.

CloudLIMS says laboratory AI use increased 14% year over year and cites a separate study in which 25.6% of laboratories had active AI projects. It does not measure US independent testing laboratories, define material use for NAICS 541380, or support a 12% adoption level.

Capstone supports the announced Acuren acquisition of diversified NV5 at 10.9x EV/EBITDA. The page does not support the quoted Acuren 9.7x or ATS 11.2x transactions, Eurofins/Intertek trading comps, a lab-only 10x-12x range, or the selected 11.5x midpoint.

The LIMS vendor article describes next-generation LIMS features and claims turnaround-time and cost reductions. It contains no 1995 adoption baseline, penetration observation, or 10-12-year path to 50% LIMS adoption.

Oberlo reports 47% Gen X and 37% boomer ownership across all US small businesses in 2024, with boomers defined as ages 55-73. It does not report age 60-plus, testing-lab owners, or evidence that supports increasing a roughly 20-22% general estimate to 35% for independent labs.

ENGEO's 2024 construction-materials/geotechnical schedule verifies ASTM method-specific unit prices, rush prices, and some hourly specimen-preparation charges for one accredited lab. It cannot establish a 70% per-test, 20% project-bid, and 10% recurring revenue mix across heterogeneous NAICS 541380 laboratories.

reasons: Replace inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://cloudlims.com/is-your-lab-data-ready-for-ai-in-2025-how-a-lims-can-help/) with a defined US independent-testing-lab survey or a reproducible estimate chain; year-over-year growth and 25.6% active projects do not support 12% material firm adoption.

Re-derive inputs.historical_analogs.value and inputs.t50_years from dated LIMS or comparable diffusion observations. https://revollims.com/next-gen-lims-ai-iot-mobile-transform-labs contains no 10-12-year adoption arc.

Support inputs.owners_60plus_pct.value with laboratory-owner evidence or a defensible age-60-plus technical-services proxy; the general Oberlo generation shares do not justify 35% for independent lab owners.
Provide one or more transaction-level citations covering each platform counted in inputs.active_consolidators; the SAM geospatial release is unrelated to the six named testing-lab consolidators.
Replace inputs.buy_mult.url with small US laboratory add-on transactions or a normalized broker dataset. The Mainsights platform outcomes do not support a 7.5x small-lab buy median.

Support inputs.exit_mult.value with directly comparable $10M-plus testing-lab platform transactions. The Capstone URL verifies NV5 at 10.9x only and does not substantiate the full 10x-12x range or 11.5x midpoint.

Substantiate inputs.pricing_structure.value and cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation with cross-subsegment revenue-mix evidence; ENGEO's single-lab fee schedule demonstrates per-test pricing but not the asserted 70/20/10 industry mix.

Add an auditable accreditation source for cross_checks.terminal_value.justification and distinguish requirements for the material 541380 subsegments instead of treating ISO 17025 human review as universal without support.
flags_reviewed: ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 3 inputs with quality ESTIMATE (current_adoption_pct, owners_60plus_pct, t50_years)
ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, M, S, V
